meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Apps and Agents Developer Associate (AI-103)
meta.title: MS-AI103-2: Search, Tools and Safe Agent Apps

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **ground a chat app on frequently updated private documents without retraining the model**?

options.text: Rely on the base model's pretraining knowledge only
Paste the full document corpus into each chat request
Fine-tune the model every time source documents change
Use Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Azure AI Search as the retrieval layer

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because RAG keeps knowledge outside model weights and retrieves current indexed content at inference time. **A** is incorrect because model pretraining does not contain organization-specific updates **B** is incorrect because large corpora exceed practical context limits and sharply increase cost **C** is incorrect because fine-tuning is slower and costlier than updating a retrieval index for changing knowledge

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **combine keyword precision with semantic/vector recall for enterprise search**?

options.text: Use only vector search with no lexical signal
Use hybrid search that combines text and vector ranking
Disable ranking and return documents in ingestion order
Use only BM25 keyword search

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because hybrid search is designed to merge lexical and vector signals so exact terms and semantic similarity both contribute. **A** is incorrect because vector-only retrieval can lose exact-term precision for identifiers or rare phrases **C** is incorrect because ingestion order does not reflect relevance **D** is incorrect because keyword-only retrieval can miss semantically relevant documents with different wording

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **let a model call approved business functions with validated argument schemas**?

options.text: Ask the model to describe the desired API call in plain text and let the user interpret it
Define tools/functions with JSON-schema-style parameters and execute them only when the model requests a tool call
Fine-tune the model instead of using structured tool definitions
Store tool instructions inside retrieved documents and hope the model follows them

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because structured tool definitions let the model produce machine-readable arguments while the application remains in control of execution. **A** is incorrect because plain-text tool suggestions are less reliable and harder to validate programmatically **C** is incorrect because fine-tuning does not give the application a controlled tool invocation protocol **D** is incorrect because retrieved prose does not replace explicit tool contracts

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **detect harmful prompts and generated content before returning it to end users**?

options.text: Rely only on temperature settings to make the model safer
Use Azure AI Content Safety and related safety controls
Shorten the system prompt so the model has less room to answer
Disable retrieval so fewer tokens are processed

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because safety services are built to classify harmful content, apply thresholds, and support responsible AI mitigations. **A** is incorrect because temperature affects randomness, not policy enforcement **C** is incorrect because prompt length is not a direct safety control **D** is incorrect because retrieval volume does not replace content moderation

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **organize models, data connections, and evaluation assets inside a managed Azure AI workspace**?

options.text: Use an Azure AI Foundry hub and project structure
Create a SQL database and place prompts there
Use only a standalone storage account
Deploy everything from a local notebook without a shared project

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Foundry hubs and projects provide the managed workspace structure for models, connections, tracing, and evaluations. **B** is incorrect because a database is not the primary AI application management surface **C** is incorrect because storage alone does not provide AI project lifecycle capabilities **D** is incorrect because local-only work does not provide the managed collaboration model expected in Foundry

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **preserve important history in a long-running assistant conversation without overflowing the model context**?

options.text: Replace the system prompt with the conversation history
Summarize older turns and keep the latest high-value details in context
Append every previous message forever
Increase temperature to make the model remember more

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because conversation summarization compresses older context while preserving salient facts within the available context window. **A** is incorrect because removing system guidance weakens control and still does not solve growth of history **C** is incorrect because unbounded history eventually exceeds context limits **D** is incorrect because temperature does not expand context capacity

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **preserve important history in a long-running assistant conversation without overflowing the model context**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Append every previous message forever
Increase temperature to make the model remember more
Summarize older turns and keep the latest high-value details in context

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **improve retrieval quality by producing coherent chunks during indexing**?

options.text: Increase generation temperature during chat completion
Disable chunk overlap and metadata completely
Store each entire manual as one vector
Split source content on semantic or natural topic boundaries

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because coherent chunks improve match quality because each retrievable unit stays focused on one concept. **A** is incorrect because generation temperature does not improve indexing quality **B** is incorrect because removing useful context and metadata can hurt retrieval quality **C** is incorrect because oversized chunks reduce precision and blur topics

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **measure groundedness, relevance, and quality of a generative AI application before release**?

options.text: Run evaluations in Azure AI Foundry
Use VM CPU utilization as the primary quality signal
Judge quality only by developer intuition after a few chats
Skip evaluation until after users report issues

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Foundry supports application evaluation workflows for quality and safety metrics such as groundedness and relevance. **B** is incorrect because infrastructure metrics do not directly measure output quality **C** is incorrect because ad hoc testing is not a reliable evaluation practice **D** is incorrect because waiting for production failures is risky and avoidable

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **reliably constrain assistant behavior with durable top-priority instructions**?

options.text: Use embeddings instead of prompt instructions
Put the governing instructions in the system message
Hope the model infers the intended policy from retrieved documents
Place all behavior rules only in user messages

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because the system message is the primary place to establish durable role, policy, and formatting constraints. **A** is incorrect because embeddings help retrieval, not instruction hierarchy **C** is incorrect because retrieved context is not a strong substitute for explicit instructions **D** is incorrect because user messages are lower-trust and easier to conflict with later prompts

questions.stem: Which TWO statements are correct about **allow the model to answer with current order status from an internal system while minimizing hallucinated order details**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Expose a tool that looks up order data from the source system at runtime
Ask the model to invent the order data if the tool is unavailable
Give the model thousands of example order numbers in the system prompt
Validate tool arguments before executing the lookup

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because runtime lookup grounds the answer in current transactional data instead of model memory **D** is correct because argument validation helps prevent malformed or unsafe tool execution **B** is incorrect because invented transactional data is an unacceptable hallucination **C** is incorrect because examples do not provide current per-user order state and waste context

questions.stem: A team is reviewing design choices for **allow the model to answer with current order status from an internal system while minimizing hallucinated order details**. Which TWO choices are valid? (Select TWO)

options.text: Expose a tool that looks up order data from the source system at runtime
Give the model thousands of example order numbers in the system prompt
Ask the model to invent the order data if the tool is unavailable
Validate tool arguments before executing the lookup

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because runtime lookup grounds the answer in current transactional data instead of model memory **D** is correct because argument validation helps prevent malformed or unsafe tool execution **B** is incorrect because examples do not provide current per-user order state and waste context **C** is incorrect because invented transactional data is an unacceptable hallucination

options.text: Expose a tool that looks up order data from the source system at runtime
Give the model thousands of example order numbers in the system prompt

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because runtime lookup grounds the answer in current transactional data instead of model memory **D** is correct because argument validation helps prevent malformed or unsafe tool execution **B** is incorrect because examples do not provide current per-user order state and waste context **C** is incorrect because invented transactional data is an unacceptable hallucination

Ask the model to invent the order data if the tool is unavailable

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because argument validation helps prevent malformed or unsafe tool execution **B** is correct because runtime lookup grounds the answer in current transactional data instead of model memory **C** is incorrect because invented transactional data is an unacceptable hallucination **D** is incorrect because examples do not provide current per-user order state and waste context
